senses.definition: the act or process of weaving or intertwining strands, commonly hair or fibers, into a braid or braids
examples: Her braidings were neatly arranged into multiple small plaits.
The artist described the braidings of the fiber art as intricate and deliberate.

senses.definition: the belief or practice related to braiding; a disorderly or informal term not widely established in standard dictionaries
examples: Braidism is not commonly recognized in modern lexicons.
The workshop explored braidism as a casual approach to hair artistry.

senses.definition: a person who braids hair; an expert in braiding
examples: The braidist styled her hair into an elaborate crown braid.
We hired a skilled braidist for the wedding party.

senses.definition: plural of braid; three or more interlaced strands of hair or fibers
examples: She wore two braids to keep her hair tidy.
The craft kit included colorful threads for braids.

senses.definition: to weave strands together to form a braid
examples: She braids her hair every morning.
They braided the rope to create a strong knot.

senses.definition: historical term for knee-length linen or cloth undergarments worn in parts of Europe in the Middle Ages
examples: Exhibits described medieval braies as practical undergarments.
The costume designer studied braies to recreate authentic period attire.

senses.definition: a mechanism on a sailing ship used for lowering or raising sails, or the rope controlling a sail’s leech
examples: The sailors adjusted the brail to reduce sail area.
He hauled on the brail to tighten the sail in the gust.

senses.definition: to haul on a brail rope to adjust a sail
examples: They brailed the sail to slow the boat.
Brailing helped the crew manage the sudden wind shift.

senses.definition: past tense of brail; hauled on a brail rope to adjust a sail
examples: The crew brailed the sail to reduce speed.
Brailed sheets indicated a controlled maneuver.

senses.definition: present participle of brail; adjusting sails by pulling brail ropes
examples: The team is brailing the sail to catch the gust.
Brailing is a routine part of reefing.

senses.definition: a system of raised dots that allows blind people to read by touch
examples: She learned Braille to read books without assistance.
The classroom has Braille labels on the signs.

senses.definition: past tense of braille; to print or emboss text in Braille
examples: The document was brailled for accessibility.
They brailled the instructions so the blind users could read them.

senses.definition: a person who writes or prints in Braille; a device that produces Braille
examples: The brailler demonstrated how to write in Braille.
The classroom has several braillers available for use.

senses.definition: plural of brailler; people or devices that produce Braille
examples: The center employs several braillers to support students.
Braillers are essential for accessible communication.

senses.definition: plural of Braille; the system of raised dots used for reading by touch
examples: He learned several brailles to help classmates.
The labels were in brailles for accessibility.

senses.definition: a device used to write Braille by embossing or punching dots
examples: The old braillewriter is still functional for practice.
Students used a braillewriter to compose notes.

senses.definition: plural of braillewriter
examples: The workshop provided several braillewriters for attendees.
Braillewriters help blind people create written text.

senses.definition: present participle of braille; creating Braille text
examples: They are brailling the pages for the blind students.
Brailling requires careful alignment of dots.

senses.definition: a person who writes Braille or works with Braille materials; a braille expert
examples: The braillist demonstrated techniques for teaching Braille.
A skilled braillist can produce clear and readable Braille.

senses.definition: plural of braillist
examples: The conference gathered several braillists from different countries.
Braillists contribute to accessibility in publishing.

senses.definition: plural of brail; rope devices on ships or openings in a furrowed fabric; in textiles, a channel or groove
examples: The ship's crew adjusted the brails to tune the sails.
The fabric features several brails along the seam.

senses.definition: the organ inside the head that controls thinking, feeling, and movement
examples: The brain processes information from the senses.
She hit her head and felt it hurt in the brain area.

senses.definition: a very smart or clever person
examples: He's a real brain when it comes to math.
She's a brain around problem solving.

senses.definition: a headache or pain in the head; figuratively, a source of worry or trouble
examples: He had a brainache after staring at the screen too long.
That math problem gives me a brainache.

senses.definition: a person who is very intelligent or a container holding a brain-like object in fiction
examples: He's a bit of a brainbox in physics class.
That brainbox solved the puzzle in seconds.

senses.definition: plural of brainbox
examples: The brainboxes discussed theories at the conference.
Two brainboxes joined the team to work on the project.

senses.definition: a cap worn to protect the head or a cap with brain-themed branding (less common)
examples: The cyclist wore a braincap for warmth during the ride.
The museum offered braincap souvenirs.

senses.definition: the skull or the part of the skull that encloses the brain
examples: The injury damaged the braincase.
The fossil shows the shape of the braincase.
